skill.name: hybrid-search-implementation
skill.description: Guides implementation of hybrid search systems that combine vector similarity, keyword matching, fusion, and reranking for stronger retrieval quality.
skill.summary: Build hybrid search for RAG and search applications using vector, keyword, fusion, and reranking patterns.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static shell, credential, weak crypto, system reconnaissance, and critical heuristic findings are false positives caused by markdown fences and search-domain terminology. One real concern remains: the PostgreSQL template builds part of a SQL filter with an interpolated metadata key, which could be unsafe if copied into production without validation.

medium_findings.title: Dynamic SQL Filter Construction in Template
medium_findings.description: The PostgreSQL example appends metadata keys directly into a SQL WHERE clause while only parameterizing values. If a caller controls metadata keys, copied code could allow SQL injection or broken query logic.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code clearly interpolates a filter key into SQL text before executing conn.fetch. Legitimate use is possible with trusted keys, but the template does not show allowlisting or identifier validation.

low_findings.title: Static Analyzer False Positives from Markdown and Domain Terms
low_findings.description: The reported shell execution locations are markdown code fences or resource references, not Ruby backticks. The sensitive, weak crypto, and system reconnaissance hits are search-domain words such as keyword, RAG systems, rank, and metadata.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line-numbered review found documentation and Python examples, not active shell commands, credential files, weak cryptography, or reconnaissance instructions. The static labels match common keyword collisions in this search-focused skill.

low_findings.title: External Documentation Links and Client Calls Require Normal Review
low_findings.description: The playbook contains links to public search resources and examples that call database, Elasticsearch, and model clients. No evidence found of credential exfiltration or unauthorized outbound requests.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The network evidence is consistent with normal hybrid search implementation examples and documentation references. I did not find hidden endpoints, encoded payloads, or secret collection.

dangerous_patterns.title: Unvalidated Identifier Interpolation
dangerous_patterns.description: The sample SQL builder interpolates metadata keys into a query string. Production implementations should allowlist metadata fields or use a safe identifier quoting API.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The unsafe pattern is visible in the template and is not mitigated nearby. The risk depends on whether metadata keys can be influenced by users.

content.value_statement: Vector search can miss exact terms, while keyword search can miss semantic matches. This skill provides practical patterns for combining both methods with fusion and reranking.
actual_capabilities: Explains when to combine vector similarity with keyword search.
Provides Reciprocal Rank Fusion and linear score combination patterns.
Shows PostgreSQL pgvector and full-text search examples.
Shows Elasticsearch dense vector and BM25 hybrid search examples.
Outlines a custom hybrid RAG pipeline with optional reranking.
Lists practical tuning, logging, and evaluation practices.
limitations: It is a guidance skill, not an installable search engine.
Examples require adaptation for each database, index, and schema.
The PostgreSQL metadata filter pattern needs hardening before production use.
It does not include benchmark datasets or automated evaluation scripts.

use_cases.title: Improve RAG Recall
use_cases.description: Combine semantic retrieval with exact term matching for better context selection.

use_cases.title: Design Search Infrastructure
use_cases.description: Compare PostgreSQL, Elasticsearch, fusion, and reranking choices before implementation.

use_cases.title: Tune Domain Search
use_cases.description: Handle names, codes, product terms, and domain vocabulary that pure vector search misses.

prompt_templates.title: Choose a Hybrid Search Design
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me choose a hybrid search design for my RAG application. My data is [describe data], and my latency target is [target].

prompt_templates.title: Implement RRF Fusion
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a Reciprocal Rank Fusion flow for vector and keyword results. Include inputs, ranking steps, and validation checks.

prompt_templates.title: Adapt PostgreSQL Search
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my PostgreSQL pgvector and full-text search plan. Identify schema, index, filtering, and SQL safety changes.

prompt_templates.title: Optimize a Production Pipeline
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this hybrid search pipeline for relevance, latency, reranking, observability, and A/B testing. Recommend production improvements.

output_examples.input: I need hybrid search for support articles with product names and error codes.
output_examples.output: A recommended design using keyword search for exact identifiers, vector search for semantic phrasing, RRF fusion, and evaluation by query class.

output_examples.input: My vector search misses customer names and SKUs.
output_examples.output: A diagnosis explaining why lexical matching is needed, plus a plan for BM25 weighting, metadata filters, and reranking.

output_examples.input: I want to compare PostgreSQL and Elasticsearch for hybrid search.
output_examples.output: A tradeoff summary covering index support, operational complexity, filtering, scaling, and reranking integration.

best_practices: Evaluate vector, keyword, fused, and reranked results separately.
Allowlist filter fields before building database queries.
Log component scores so ranking changes can be debugged.
anti_patterns: Do not assume one vector model solves all exact-match queries.
Do not copy SQL templates without validating identifiers and filters.
Do not tune weights without representative queries and relevance labels.

faq.question: What does this skill help build?
faq.answer: It helps design hybrid search systems that combine vector similarity, keyword search, fusion, and reranking.

faq.question: Does it run a search service?
faq.answer: No. It provides implementation guidance and templates that must be adapted to your stack.

faq.question: Which backends are covered?
faq.answer: The playbook includes examples for PostgreSQL with pgvector, Elasticsearch, and a custom RAG pipeline.

faq.question: Is the PostgreSQL example production ready?
faq.answer: Not directly. Validate or allowlist metadata filter keys before using that pattern in production.

faq.question: When should I use reranking?
faq.answer: Use reranking when higher relevance matters and your latency budget can support an extra model call.

faq.question: Can Claude, Codex, or Claude Code use it?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill metadata lists Claude, Codex, and Claude Code as supported tools.
